payload.source_text:
QUALITY POLICY STATEMENT
Novus Mechanical Services Limited has established a Quality Policy to be consistent with the purpose and context of our organisation.

It provides a framework for the setting and review of objectives in addition to our commitment to satisfy applicable customers’, regulatory and legislative requirements as well as our commitment to continually improve our management system.
Customer focus: As an organisation we have made a commitment to understand our current and future customers’ needs; meet their requirements and strive to exceed their expectations.
Leadership: Our Top Management have committed to creating and maintaining a working environment in which people become fully involved in achieving our objectives.
Engagement of people: As an organisation we recognise that people are the essence of any good business and that their full involvement enables their abilities to be used for our benefit.

Process approach: As an organisation we understand that a desired result is achieved more efficiently when activities and related resources are managed as a process or series of interconnected processes.
Improvement: We have committed to achieving continual improvement across all aspects of our quality management system; it is one of our main annual objectives.
Evidence-based decision making: As an organisation we have committed to only make decisions relating to our QMS following an analysis of relevant data and information.

Relationship management: We recognises that an organisation and the relationship it has with its external providers are interdependent and a mutually beneficial relationship enhances the ability of both to create value.
Our policy is also to meet the requirements of our social, environmental, charitable and legislative responsibilities.

Signed : 							Date: 22/4/2025
           	
Damian Shacklock
Director
NOVUS MECHANICAL SERVICES LTD.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Quality objective-setting function
payload.justification: SG279 defines quality policy framework purpose as enabling objective-setting function within the organization.

payload.description: Process approach for achieving organizational results
payload.justification: SG283 declares process approach as the organizational method for achieving desired results with efficiency.

payload.description: Evidence-based decision making process
payload.justification: SG285 commits the organization to evidence-based decision making as a governance process principle.

payload.description: Continual improvement across quality management
payload.justification: SG284 establishes continual improvement as a core process executed annually across the organization.

payload.description: Quality policy framework
payload.justification: SG278 and SG279 establish quality policy as a foundational governance framework entity aligned with business purpose and regulatory compliance.

payload.description: Quality policy alignment with organizational business purpose and context
payload.justification: SG278 explicitly establishes the relationship between quality policy and business purpose/context as foundational governance.

payload.description: Customer needs and expectations
payload.justification: SG280 articulates customer needs and expectations as a strategic entity that the organization must understand and exceed.

payload.description: Work environment enabling objective achievement
payload.justification: SG281 identifies work environment as an entity that leadership must create to enable organizational objectives.

payload.description: External provider relationships
payload.justification: SG286 recognizes external provider relationships as strategic entities for value creation.

payload.description: Annual quality management improvement cycle
payload.justification: SG284 establishes continual improvement as a core annual cycle within the organization.

payload.description: Organizational director with policy authorization authority
payload.justification: SG288 explicitly identifies the organizational director role as the formal policy authorization authority.

payload.description: Leadership creating enabling work environment
payload.justification: SG281 expresses leadership as the actor responsible for creating the enabling work environment.

payload.description: Engaged people as strategic enablers
payload.justification: SG282 recognizes people engagement as an essential organizational value, identifying people as key strategic actors.

payload.description: Understanding and exceeding customer expectations
payload.justification: SG280 articulates strategic commitment to understanding customer needs and exceeding expectations as an organizational driver and goal.

payload.description: Achieving organizational objectives through enabled workforce
payload.justification: SG281 expresses the goal of achieving organizational objectives through creating an enabling work environment.

payload.description: People engagement as essential organizational value
payload.justification: SG282 recognizes people engagement as an essential organizational value and principle enabling strategic benefit.

payload.description: Process approach for organizational efficiency
payload.justification: SG283 declares process approach as an organizational efficiency principle for achieving desired results.

payload.description: Continual improvement of quality management system
payload.justification: SG284 establishes continual improvement as a core organizational objective.

payload.description: Evidence-based decision making for quality management
payload.justification: SG285 commits the organization to evidence-based decision making as a governance principle.

payload.description: Mutually beneficial external provider value creation
payload.justification: SG286 recognizes mutually beneficial external provider relationships as a strategic driver and goal for value creation.

payload.description: Commitment to regulatory compliance requirements
payload.justification: SG279 defines commitment to regulatory compliance as a constraint within the quality policy framework purpose.

payload.description: Social responsibility obligations
payload.justification: SG287 declares commitment to social responsibilities as an organizational constraint and obligation.

payload.description: Environmental responsibility obligations
payload.justification: SG287 declares commitment to environmental responsibilities as an organizational constraint and obligation.

payload.description: Charitable responsibility obligations
payload.justification: SG287 declares commitment to charitable responsibilities as an organizational constraint and obligation.

payload.description: Legislative compliance obligations
payload.justification: SG287 declares commitment to legislative responsibilities as an organizational constraint and obligation.

payload.description: Customer focus process for understanding and meeting needs
payload.justification: SG449 articulates customer focus as requiring understanding needs, meeting requirements, and exceeding expectations—describing a business process.

payload.description: Process approach for managing interconnected activities and resources
payload.justification: SG452 establishes process approach as managing activities and resources as interconnected processes, describing a meta-process workflow.

payload.description: Continual improvement process across quality management system
payload.justification: SG453 defines continual improvement as a central business objective, implying an ongoing improvement process.

payload.description: Evidence-based decision making process
payload.justification: SG454 describes data analysis before decisions as a process requirement for quality management system decisions.

payload.description: Relationship management process with external providers
payload.justification: SG455 defines relationship management as a conceptual model, implying a business process for managing external provider relationships.

payload.description: Data analysis must precede quality management system decisions
payload.justification: SG454 explicitly establishes evidence-based decision making as a business rule requiring data analysis before decisions.

payload.description: Activities and resources must be managed as interconnected processes
payload.justification: SG452 establishes the process approach as a conceptual model requiring interconnection, representing a business rule.

payload.description: Quality policy as a documented business entity
payload.justification: SG447 and SG448 establish quality policy as a foundational entity with defined scope and framework characteristics at the business context level.

payload.description: Customer requirement as a business data object
payload.justification: SG448 identifies customer requirements as part of the policy framework; SG449 establishes the need to understand and meet customer requirements.

payload.description: Customer expectation as a business data object
payload.justification: SG449 distinguishes customer expectations from requirements, establishing them as a distinct entity to be exceeded.

payload.description: Quality management system as a business entity
payload.justification: SG453 and SG454 reference the quality management system as a central business entity subject to improvement and decision-making.

payload.description: Quality policy encompasses quality objectives
payload.justification: SG448 defines the policy framework as encompassing objective-setting, establishing a containment relationship.

payload.description: External provider relationship creates mutual value
payload.justification: SG455 explicitly defines interdependent relationships with external providers that mutually create value.

payload.description: Continual improvement cycle across all quality aspects
payload.justification: SG453 defines continual improvement as central and ongoing, implying a recurring business cycle rather than discrete events.

payload.description: Customer as a business actor with needs and expectations
payload.justification: SG448 and SG449 identify customers as actors with requirements, needs, and expectations that must be understood and met.

payload.description: External provider as a business actor in value-creating relationships
payload.justification: SG455 identifies external providers as actors in interdependent relationships that mutually create value.

payload.description: Leadership role responsible for creating enabling environments
payload.justification: SG450 defines leadership as the conceptual responsibility for creating environments enabling objective achievement, describing a business role.

payload.description: People engagement role as organizational capability contributors
payload.justification: SG451 identifies people engagement as recognizing human capabilities as essential assets, describing a role concept.

payload.description: Achieve customer satisfaction by meeting and exceeding expectations
payload.justification: SG449 articulates customer focus as meeting requirements and exceeding expectations, representing a core business goal.

payload.description: Enable objective achievement through people involvement
payload.justification: SG450 defines leadership's purpose as creating environments that enable objective achievement through people involvement.

payload.description: Achieve efficiency through process interconnection
payload.justification: SG452 establishes the process approach as aimed at efficiency through managing interconnected processes.

payload.description: Continually improve quality management system performance
payload.justification: SG453 explicitly defines continual improvement as a central business objective across all quality management system aspects.

payload.description: Create mutual value through external provider relationships
payload.justification: SG455 establishes mutual value creation as the purpose of managing interdependent external provider relationships.

payload.description: Quality policy must align with organizational purpose and context
payload.justification: SG447 establishes alignment with organizational purpose and context as a foundational principle for quality policy.

payload.description: Decisions must be based on analyzed data and evidence
payload.justification: SG454 establishes evidence-based decision making as a business principle requiring data analysis foundation.

payload.description: Human capabilities are essential organizational assets
payload.justification: SG451 establishes people engagement as recognizing human capabilities as essential assets, representing a core business principle.

payload.description: Quality policy must address regulatory compliance and legislative responsibility requirements
payload.justification: SG448 defines the policy framework as encompassing regulatory compliance, establishing it as a mandatory constraint. | SG456 extends policy scope to include legislative responsibility, establishing it as a mandatory constraint. | SG456 extends policy scope to include social responsibility, establishing it as a required constraint. | SG456 extends policy scope to include environmental responsibility, establishing it as a required constraint. | SG456 extends policy scope to include charitable responsibility, establishing it as a required constraint.

payload.description: Process approach principle stating that activities and resources achieve results through interconnected processes
payload.justification: SG463 explicitly identifies a 'process approach principle' articulating a logical management philosophy. This is a design principle governing how the QMS conceptually organizes work. Clear match to Why/Principle at the Designer abstraction level.

payload.description: Evidence-based decision making principle requiring QMS decisions to follow from analysis of data and information
payload.justification: SG464 explicitly identifies an 'evidence-based decision making principle' establishing a logical requirement for decision-making approach. This is a design principle constraining how decisions are made within the QMS. Clear match to Why/Principle at the Designer abstraction level.

payload.description: Temporal validity period attribute of policy document
payload.justification: Signal mentions 'temporal validity' as a property of the policy document. At Row 5, this represents a detailed data attribute specification defining the time-bound nature of document authority.

payload.description: Policy document entity with signature block metadata
payload.justification: Signal describes a policy document structure containing signature block. At Row 5, this represents the detailed data definition of the document entity that carries organizational authority markers.

payload.description: Director role with signature authority for policy document approval
payload.justification: Signal describes a Director signature block indicating organizational authority. At Row 5 (Implementer), this represents the detailed role specification with signature authorization rights. Classification_type 'Role' is appropriate for organizational authority positions.

payload.description: Organizational authority requirement for policy document validity
payload.justification: Signal implies that Director signature provides organizational authority, representing a validation constraint. At Row 5, this is a detailed rule governing what constitutes valid policy authorization.

payload.description: Establishes organizational quality policy aligned with business purpose and context as foundational governance framework.
Defines quality policy framework purpose for objective-setting and commitment to regulatory compliance and continuous improvement.
Articulates strategic commitment to understanding customer needs and exceeding expectations as organizational driver.
Expresses leadership commitment to creating enabling work environment for achieving organizational objectives.
Recognizes people engagement as essential organizational value and strategic enabler for business benefit.
Declares process approach as organizational efficiency principle for achieving desired results.
Establishes continual improvement across quality management as core annual organizational objective.
Commits organization to evidence-based decision making principle for quality management governance.
Recognizes mutually beneficial external provider relationships as strategic driver for value creation.
Declares policy commitment to social, environmental, charitable, and legislative responsibilities as organizational obligations.
Provides formal policy authorization by organizational director establishing governance authority and accountability.
Establishes the conceptual foundation for a quality policy aligned with organizational purpose and context.
Defines the policy framework as encompassing objective-setting, customer requirements, regulatory compliance, and continual improvement commitments.
Articulates customer focus as a business concept requiring understanding of needs, meeting requirements, and exceeding expectations.
Defines leadership as the conceptual responsibility for creating environments that enable objective achievement through people involvement.
Identifies people engagement as a core business concept recognizing human capabilities as essential organizational assets.
Establishes process approach as a conceptual model where activities and resources are managed as interconnected processes for efficiency.
Defines continual improvement as a central business objective across all quality management system aspects.
Establishes evidence-based decision making as a business rule requiring data analysis before quality management system decisions.
Defines relationship management as a conceptual model of interdependent external provider relationships that mutually create value.
Extends policy scope to encompass social, environmental, charitable, and legislative responsibility requirements.
Process approach principle articulating logical management philosophy that activities and resources achieve results through process or interconnected process series.
Evidence-based decision making principle establishing logical requirement that QMS decisions follow data and information analysis.
Director signature block identifying specific organizational authority and temporal validity of policy document.
